stem: A GPS navigation navigation system uses 2 parallel satellite links for redundancy. The position fixed requires at least one satellite link to function. satellite link 1 has a reliability between 70% and 83% (uncertain due to limited operational data). satellite link 2 has a reliability of 80%. What is the overall probability that the position fixed?

ground_truth.answer: P(position fixed) ranges from 0.94 to 0.966. Point estimate: 0.952.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 2 satellite links fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.3 × 0.2 = 0.06. P(works) = 0.94.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.17 × 0.2 = 0.034. P(works) = 0.966.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
